narrative: Employee in the Machine Shop received second degree burns to the back of his legs after the torch he was using exploded.

EVENT.title: Ignition of vapors, gases, or liquids

narrative: An employee was disassembling a wire-line tool when a section of the tooling fell onto the employee's hand. The employee sustained deep lacerations to four fingers and small fractures to two fingers, requiring hospitalization.

EVENT.title: Struck by object falling from vehicle or machinery-other than vehicle part

narrative: An employee sustained severe lacerations to the pinky and ring fingers of his left hand while cutting bone in pork on a band saw.

EVENT.title: Struck against moving part of machinery or equipment

narrative: An employee was working with the basket mill in the production area and severely lacerated his left thumb. The employee was hospitalized and received surgery on his finger.

EVENT.title: Caught in running equipment or machinery during regular operation

narrative: An employee suffered three fingertip amputations while unclogging a sandblaster.

EVENT.title: Caught in running equipment or machinery during maintenance, cleaning

narrative: While separating bundles of steel joist, the joist became stuck and an employee used a pry bar to dislodge the joist. The employee attempted to separate the joist with his hands. The joist shifted and pinched the employee's right index finger resulting in partial amputation.

EVENT.title: Compressed or pinched by shifting objects or equipment

narrative: An employee was using a portable band saw to create quality samples from steel tubing. He held the band saw with his right hand and attempted to catch the sample piece with his left. The saw blade bound up inside the tubing. He was attempting to steady the saw with his left hand when the blade cut and amputated his left index fingertip.

EVENT.title: Injured by slipping or swinging object held by injured worker

narrative: An employee was using a reach truck to store material on a rack. The employee was standing on the forklift and the employee's left foot was caught between the frame of the forklift and the rack resulting in a crushing injury. The left foot was surgically amputated.

EVENT.title: Part of occupant s body caught between vehicle and other object in nonroadway transport incident

narrative: On October 17, 2017, three employees were testing the voltage of electrical equipment that was not labeled as high voltage during Hurricane Maria operations when an arc flash occurred. One of the employees suffered burns that required hospitalization.

EVENT.title: Direct exposure to electricity, greater than 220 volts

narrative: An employee was operating a lathe when the employee's left arm was caught in the lathe, resulting in a left arm amputation.

EVENT.title: Caught in running equipment or machinery during regular operation

narrative: An employee with Peninsula Roofing Company, Inc., slipped and fell approximately 26' as he was transitioning from a ladder to a roof with his personal fall arrest system harness. He suffered fractured wrists, a broken heel and right foot, and a small fracture to the spine near the tailbone, requiring hospitalization.

EVENT.title: Other fall to lower level 26 to 30 feet

narrative: While moving freight, an employee tripped and fell from a trailer to the ground about 5 feet below. The employee suffered a fractured right elbow.

EVENT.title: Other fall to lower level less than 6 feet

narrative: An employee had chest muscle pains after finishing snow blowing. The snowblowers reverse gear was not working properly.

PART_OF_BODY.title: Chest, except internal location of diseases or disorders

EVENT.title: Overexertion in pushing, pulling, or turning-single episode

narrative: An employee entered the newly remodeled fitness room, slipped on the wet concrete floor and fell breaking their pelvis.

EVENT.title: Fall on same level due to slipping

narrative: An employee was electric shocked while plugging a warmer machine into an outlet.

EVENT.title: Direct exposure to electricity, unspecified

narrative: An employee was cutting meat with a band saw when his right middle finger contacted the saw blade, resulting in a soft tissue amputation to the finger.

EVENT.title: Struck against moving part of machinery or equipment

narrative: An employee was spackling from a baker's scaffold about 18-20 feet above a concrete floor. He fell, struck the ground feet first, and rolled, suffering muscle strains throughout his body.

EVENT.title: Other fall to lower level 16 to 20 feet

narrative: An employee was operating the snubbing unit snubbing a pipe out of the hole and laying down pipe using a single latch elevator on the driller side rig winch. The elevator got hung up on the tubing board above and the latch on the elevator opened allowing the pipe joint to come free, fall, and strike the employee in the forehead. The employee was hospitalized.

EVENT.title: Struck by falling object or equipment, unspecified

narrative: An employee was using a ladder to access a racking system. He fell, suffering injuries to the head and face.

EVENT.title: Other fall to lower level, unspecified

narrative: Two employees were transporting a cold box with a self-propelled modular transporter. As the load passed a power pole with a distribution transformer, the transformer arced onto a chain securing the load. The current traveled through the equipment and shocked both employees.

EVENT.title: Indirect exposure to electricity, unspecified

SOURCE.title: Material and personnel handling machinery, n.e.c.

narrative: On 1/4/2022 at 11:55 p.m., an employee completed the plating of the second side of a floor truss while working as an assembler on a floor line. The employee reached toward the top plate of an assembler to correct an alignment issue when the assembler actuated the air valve and closed the jig. The employee's gloved left hand was on top of the truss and the jig rail closed on his index and middle fingertips, resulting in amputations.

EVENT.title: Caught in or compressed by equipment or objects, unspecified

SOURCE.title: Metal, woodworking, and special material machinery, n.e.c.

narrative: An employee was working as a yard driver at the back dock of a facility. The employee developed dehydration and kidney failure and was hospitalized for heat exhaustion.

NATURE.title: Multiple effects of heat and light

EVENT.title: Exposure to environmental heat

narrative: An employee was making a residential delivery when he slipped and fell in the rear of the trailer. The employee was hospitalized with a fractured right hip.

EVENT.title: Fall on same level due to slipping

narrative: An employee was using a 3-point auger when they were caught in the machine and hospitalized.

EVENT.title: Caught in running equipment or machinery during regular operation

narrative: An employee was measuring a coil that was on a roller conveyor. As the employee placed the tape measure on the coil, the coil passed over a roller and the employee s left index finger was pinched between the coil and the roller. The employee required surgery to amputate 4 millimeters of the fingertip.

EVENT.title: Caught in running equipment or machinery during regular operation

narrative: An employee was moving a crosshead assembly. The joint on the assembly caught his left middle finger, amputating the tip.

EVENT.title: Caught in or compressed by equipment or objects, unspecified

narrative: An employee had been welding an aluminum roof and began feeling dizzy, lightheaded and became ill due to dehydration.

EVENT.title: Exposure to environmental heat  outdoor

narrative: A contractor employee was performing maintenance on an injection molder machine when their right index finger got caught in the machine, resulting in a fingertip amputation.

EVENT.title: Struck by running powered equipment  during maintenance, cleaning, testing

narrative: An employee was walking to her car after her shift. She walked between two vehicles to enter her vehicle when she slipped on ice and fell to the ground, suffering injuries to her left knee and elbow. She was hospitalized.

EVENT.title: Fall on same level due to slip or trip

narrative: An employee was working on a rig crew. They were positioned on the rig work floor, helping to connect tubing to a power swivel. While the employee was making a horizontal connection, their left hand was positioned on the winch line that was secured to the power swivel. As the swivel rotated, the winch line chain became tangled, pinching the employee's left little finger and resulting in an amputation.

EVENT.title: Caught, entangled in running powered equipment  normal operation
